status: documentation only; no mapper code changes
purpose: Decode the 2012 and 2008 codebooks, identify safe items per PRISM target, and produce resolver-table snippets ready for mechanical wiring.
explicitly_not_in_this_audit: No engine / selector / topK / browser / dist / output / live-data / candidate / era-context edits
No edits to generated smoke outputs
forbidden_universal_inputs: pid7 / CC307a (only as flagged signal for moralBoundaries.political_camp)
sequencing_recommendation: 2012 first (modern-CCES-style names, direct cross-cycle parallels with 2016+), 2008 second (CC*** + V### archaeology). 2008 has NO immigration items — CU/national stays at fallback regardless.

items.exact_wording: Which one of the opinions on this page best agrees with your view on abortion?
items.polarity: 1 → CD high, 4 → CD low (linear monotonic)

items.exact_wording: Do you favor or oppose allowing gays and lesbians to marry legally?
items.polarity: Favor → CD low; Oppose → CD high

items.exact_wording: Birth Control Exemption. A Bill to let employers and insurers refuse to cover birth control and other health services that violate their religious beliefs.
items.polarity: Support → CD high (employer-religious-conscience); Oppose → CD low
items.reason_reduced: Religious-liberty cross-load mirrors CC16_332d employer-conscience reduced-weight rationale

items.exact_wording: End Don't Ask, Don't Tell. Would allow gays to serve openly in the armed services.
items.polarity: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high

items.exact_wording: Grant legal status to all illegal immigrants who have held jobs and paid taxes for at least 3 years, and not been convicted of any felony crimes
items.polarity: Yes → CU high (pluralist)

items.exact_wording: Increase the number of border patrols on the US-Mexican border
items.polarity: Yes → CU low + national.salience high

items.exact_wording: Allow police to question anyone they think may be in the country illegally
items.polarity: Yes → CU low; light ethnic_racial cross-load

items.exact_wording: Fine US businesses that hire illegal immigrants
items.polarity: Yes → CU low (mixed; some pro-business R Oppose, some pro-labor D Support)

items.exact_wording: Prohibit illegal immigrants from using emergency hospital care and public schools
items.polarity: Yes → CU low + national.salience high + MOR low (narrow circle)

items.exact_wording: Deny automatic citizenship to American-born children of illegal immigrants
items.polarity: Yes → CU low + national.salience VERY high
items.notes: Cleanest national-circle marker in any audited cycle

items.exact_wording: 2011 House Budget Plan (Ryan). The Budget plan would cut Medicare and Medicaid by 42%. Would reduce debt by 16% by 2020.
items.polarity: Support → MAT high (small-government / cut social spending)

items.exact_wording: Simpson-Bowles Budget Plan. Plan would make 15% cuts across the board in Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, and Defense, as well as other programs.
items.polarity: Support → MAT high; mixed because cuts include Defense (some progressive Support)

items.exact_wording: The Tax Hike Prevention Act. Would extend Bush-era tax cuts for all individuals, regardless of income.
items.polarity: Support → MAT high (preserve Bush tax cuts)

items.exact_wording: Repeal Affordable Care Act. Would repeal the Affordable Care Act.
items.polarity: Support → MAT high (anti-government healthcare)

items.exact_wording: Affordable Care Act of 2010. Requires all Americans to obtain health insurance. Allows people to keep current provider. Sets up health insurance option for those without coverage. Increases taxes on those making more than $280,000 a year.
items.polarity: INVERSE of CC332G — Support → MAT low (pro-government healthcare); Oppose → MAT high
items.implementation_critical_note: CC332G repeals ACA; CC332I passes ACA. Composite must NOT route them with the same signal_fn.

items.exact_wording: Some people think it is important to protect the environment even if it costs some jobs or otherwise reduces our standard of living. Other people think that protecting the environment is not as important as maintaining jobs and our standard of living. Which is closer to the way you feel?
items.polarity: 1 → ZS low; 5 → ZS high

items.exact_wording: U.S.-Korea Free Trade Agreement. Would remove tariffs on imports and exports between South Korea and the U.S.
items.polarity: Support → ZS low (trade as positive-sum); Oppose → ZS high

items.exact_wording: Would you say that OVER THE PAST YEAR the nation's economy has...?
items.polarity: 5 (much worse) → ZS high; 1 (much better) → ZS low

items_rejected.reason: Direction-ambiguous (Middle Class Tax Cut Act extending Bush cuts < $200K — both progressives preserving and conservatives extending Support)
Keystone Pipeline — environment / ONT_S cross-load; not pure MAT or ZS
Party ID variants — usable only for moralBoundaries.political_camp via existing pid7 wiring

section_2008.no_immigration_items: 2008 has NO immigration policy items in common content (only demographic 'are you an immigrant'). CU + national.salience stay at fallback regardless of resolver work.

items.exact_wording: Which one of the opinions on this page best agrees with your view on abortion?
items.polarity: 1 → CD high, 4 → CD low

items.exact_wording: Stem Cell Research (federal funding expansion)
items.polarity: Support → CD low (progressive bioethics); Oppose → CD high

items.exact_wording: Constitutional Amendment banning Gay Marriage
items.polarity: Support → CD HIGH (banning marriage = traditionalist); Oppose → CD low. INVERTED relative to CC326 in 2012 (which asks 'favor allowing').
items.implementation_critical_note: CC316f and CC326 have OPPOSITE direction signs. Mapper resolver must NOT share a signal_fn between them.

items.exact_wording: A proposal has been made that would allow people to put a portion of their Social Security payroll taxes into personal retirement accounts that would be invested in private stocks and bonds. Do you favor or oppose this idea?
items.polarity: 1 → MAT high (privatization); 4 → MAT low

items.exact_wording: Increase Minimum Wage from $5.15 to $7.25
items.polarity: Support → MAT low; Oppose → MAT high

items.exact_wording: Health Insurance Program for Children (S-CHIP expansion)
items.polarity: Support → MAT low (pro-government healthcare expansion)

items.exact_wording: Federal assistance for homeowners facing foreclosure and large lending institutions at risk of failing
items.polarity: Support → MAT low (pro-intervention)
items.reason_reduced: Mixed: some Support for institutional stability, not necessarily pro-redistribution

items.exact_wording: Some people think it is important to protect the environment even if it costs some jobs... (verbatim identical to CC325 in 2012)
items.polarity: 1 → ZS low; 5 → ZS high

items.exact_wording: Extend the North American Free trade Agreement (NAFTA) to include Peru and Columbia
items.code_pattern: support_oppose_2way (with Not Sure=3; HIGH Not-Sure rate ~30%)
items.polarity: Support → ZS low; Oppose → ZS high

items.exact_wording: Would you say that OVER THE PAST YEAR the nation's economy has...? (same wording / codes as 2012 CC302)
items.polarity: 5 → ZS high; 1 → ZS low

items.exact_wording: Affirmative action programs give preference to racial minorities and to women in employment and college admissions in order to correct for discrimination. Do you support or oppose affirmative action?
items.polarity: Strongly support → ethnic_racial high salience (one-sided); opposing AA does NOT push ethnic_racial low

items.exact_wording: The scales below represent the ideological spectrum from very liberal (0) to very conservative (100). (Self-placement)
items.polarity: Salience strength via distance from center (50)
items.notes: Direct 2008 equivalent of ideo5 strength used in modern cycles

items.exact_wording: Born Again (Pew version)
items.polarity: Yes → religious salience high
items.notes: Direct 2008 equivalent of pew_bornagain in modern cycles

items.exact_wording: Importance of religion (Pew version)
items.polarity: 1 (Very important) → religious salience high

items.exact_wording: Church attendance (Pew version)
items.code_pattern: ordinal_6pt (More than once a week → Never)
items.polarity: 1 (More than once a week) → religious salience high

items.exact_wording: Pre Election 7 point Party ID
items.polarity: Salience strength via distance from center (4)
items.notes: Direct 2008 equivalent of pid7 in modern cycles

items_rejected.reason: Withdraw Troops Iraq — foreign-policy / national-security framing; not a clean PRISM-target signal

Eavesdrop overseas without court order — clean PRO low on Support, BUT PRO is structurally ANES-required per hard-node audit; single-cycle item too thin to base PRO composite on. Defer to v0.1G as 'PRO 2008-only experimental'.
Bank Bailout — mixed (Support for stability vs Oppose for moral hazard); weak MAT/ZS
Ideology placement of others (parties, candidates) — close to candidate thermometer territory; potentially circular for vote prediction

new_signal_fns_required.name: cc324_abortion_likert_4pt
new_signal_fns_required.definition: (2.5 - response_value) / 1.5 clipped to [-1, +1]; missing → 0; reusable across CC310 and CC324

new_signal_fns_required.name: cc313_aa_one_sided
new_signal_fns_required.definition: max(0, (2.5 - response_value) / 1.5) — only AA-Support contributes; one-sided

implementation_order_recommendation: 1. 2012 resolver block first (~14 items across 6 composites; column names align with standard CCES naming the loader already reads). Expected smoke pass: MAT/CD/CU/national/gender/ZS for 2012 lift to 90%+.
2. 2008 issue items second (CC310, CC312, CC316b-i — 9 items across 4 composites). MAT/CD/ZS/gender lift.
3. 2008 boundary aliases third (V215/V216/V217 → religious; CC307a → political_camp; CC317a → ideological). Column-name aliasing layer, not new signal_fn.
4. 2008 ethnic_racial single-item proxy fourth (CC313). Lower priority because reduced-confidence even with resolver.
Each step ships independently; no dependency chain.
terminology: moralBoundaries.* and engagement are canonical per ADR-006/ADR canon. Engagement is referenced as a separate 1D continuous scalar. Legacy code identifiers (pid7, ideo5, CC*_*, V###, pew_*) appear only as CCES variable names.

revision_history.description: Initial 2008/2012 resolver audit. 23 items spec'd across both cycles, 7 new signal_fn types required, 14 (target × cycle) cells projected to lift from blocker to covered/reduced after wiring.
